aggregator-sources.AddSourceModal.InfoMe: The calendar has been added to your sources. The events are being evaluated, those which correspond to the rules that you have defined will go up in your calendar in a few minutes.
The calendar has been added to your sources. The next events which will be published there and which correspond to the rules that you have defined will go up in your calendar.
aggregator-sources.AddSourceModal.evalua: The agenda {source} is about to be added to the sources of {aggregator} {ruleCount, plural, =0 {without rule} one {with 1 rule} other {with {ruleCount} rules}}.
aggregator-sources.AddSourceModal.modalT: Add a source{agenda, select, undefined {} other {: {agenda}}}
aggregator-sources.AddSourceModal.noAgen: No agenda corresponds to your search
aggregator-sources.AddSourceModal.remove: Are you sure you want to delete this agenda from sources ?
aggregator-sources.Dashboard.aggregation: You have reached the aggregation threshold of {limit} events/year of the free version !{br}To increase it, write us a message.{br}{br}<support-link>Increase the threshold</support-link>
Use the event Aggregation function to automatically resume events published on other OpenAgenda calendars. Combine up to 365 events per year with the free version!
Connect source agendas and automatize event publications
aggregator-sources.Dashboard.aggregatorE: This feature allows you to automatically fetch the events added to the agendas of your choice.
aggregator-sources.Dashboard.noSources: Your agenda is not yet linked to any sources. Add a first source agenda!
aggregator-sources.Dashboard.sourcesExpl: The events published by the agendas listed here are automatically added to the [{title}]({link}) agenda.
aggregator-sources.Dashboard.sourcesHelp: Click here to find out more about aggregations
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.actionsAf: The following values are assigned to the event when the filter matches
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.actionsDe: The following actions are always assigned to the aggregated event
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.aggregato: Field & Value of "{agendaTitle}" that will be associated with event
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.automatic: Value association is made based on labels of the field of the same name in the source agenda
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.brokenFil: this filter refers to a field that cannot be found. It may have been deleted from the source agenda!
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.descripti: Rules are applied to the events in their aggregation.{br} They are used to condition the aggregation, or to assign values to extended fields in your agenda.
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.displayAg: {count, plural, =1 {One general rule exists} other {{count} general rules exist}}
General rules apply on all events before source-specific rules are evaluated.
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.eventLoca: {geo} where the event should take place
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.filterDet: If the filter matches the event, the associated actions apply
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.manualPas: Paste rules from another source with CTRL + V
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.missingRe: {fieldsCount, plural, =1 {The field {fields} is required} other {The fields {fields} are required}}.
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.name: Location name
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.replacing: This value will come replace any other that was defined in a previous rule
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.requiredF: {fieldsCount, plural, =1 {The field {fields} is required} other {The fields {fields} are required}}.

The agenda "{agendaTitle}" has required fields. Event aggregation will only occur for events associated to at least one value of each required field. Use aggregation rules to attribute values to the event at aggregation.
This rule blocks the aggregation if the filter does not match
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.ruleDescr: A rule consists of a filter and/or actions applied to each event published on the source.
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.sourceAge: Label of an additional field of the source "{agendaTitle}"
aggregator-sources.DefineRules.uselessRu: Please define at least a required filter or one action
An aggregation rule with filter not required must have at least one action
aggregator-sources.RemoveSourceModal.eva: Yes, but I want to keep events already aggregated
Yes, and I also want to remove already aggregated events
aggregator-sources.RemoveSourceModal.rem: Events that will be published on the agenda {agenda} will no longer be aggregated.{br} Are you sure you want to delete this agenda from sources ?
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.automaticDes: The values of the field will be defined automatically according to the values read from the source.
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.clearAssignm: Replace values set by previous rules
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.clearDescrip: Any value assigned to this field to a rule executed before the current rule is replaced by the value set here
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.helpFilterCh: Apply the rule to events corresponding to one or more values coming from additional fields of the source.
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.helpFilterLo: Apply the rule to events corresponding to one or more cities, departments or regions.
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.helpFilterTa: Apply the rule to events associated with optional values whose labels correspond.
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.helpFilterTe: Apply the rule to events corresponding to one value coming from text fields of the source.
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.locationFilt: {required, select, true {Required location filter} other {Location filter}}
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.name: Name
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.requiredFilt: Required filter: Aggregation only occurs if the event matches the criteria for this filter.
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.tagFilter: {required, select, true {Required tag filter} other {Tag filter}}
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.useActionsDe: When applicable, the rule associates the aggregated event with values specific to the aggregator calendar.
aggregator-sources.RuleForm.useFilterDes: The rule only applies to events that match the defined filter.
aggregator-sources.RulesSummary.rulesSum: {count, plural, =0 {No general rule is defined} =1 {1 general rule is defined} other {# general rules are defined}}
aggregator-sources.SourcesList.update: {rulesCount, plural, =1 {See the aggregation rule} other {See the # aggregation rules}}
aggregator-sources.UpdateSourceModal.eva: Would you like events published in the source calendar to be re-evaluated as a result of this update?
